models.purpose: Combine retail text context with structured signals to classify stocking priority in a practical, explainable way.
models.inputs: Column-aware text template plus numeric retail and supply-chain features.
models.outputs: Predicted stocking-priority class with class probabilities.
models.strengths: Best class-balanced benchmark, interpretable, stable in FastAPI, and easy to maintain.
models.limitations: Still linear, so it may miss nonlinear patterns that a deeper architecture can capture.
models.deployment_relevance: Chosen for deployment because it balances performance, interpretability, and operational stability.
models.benchmark_source: Recomputed from the Week 4 notebook design on the local dataset.

models.purpose: Benchmark how much the structured operational and pricing variables can explain priority without text context.
models.inputs: Structured numeric retail, pricing, and operations features only.
models.outputs: Predicted stocking-priority class with class probabilities.
models.strengths: Business-readable and highlights the value of numeric demand and supply signals.
models.limitations: Cannot learn semantic differences between brands, sub-categories, or promotions from text.
models.deployment_relevance: Strong baseline for structured operations, but weaker than the hybrid approach.
models.benchmark_source: Recomputed from the Week 4 notebook design on the local dataset.

models.purpose: Test whether a custom hybrid neural architecture can capture richer nonlinear interactions between text and numeric features.
models.inputs: Column-aware text template and the numeric feature block.
models.strengths: Can capture nonlinear interactions and came close to the best weighted F1 while posting the highest accuracy.
models.limitations: Needs TensorFlow/Keras runtime support, more tuning, and more careful production hardening.
models.deployment_relevance: Excellent research model, but heavier and less transparent than the chosen deployed model.

models.purpose: Benchmark how much category, product, brand, and promotion text alone can explain stocking priority.
models.outputs: Predicted stocking-priority class with class probabilities.
models.strengths: Fast, simple, interpretable baseline for semantic retail context.
models.limitations: Misses shelf life, fill rate, lead time, and other structured business signals.
models.deployment_relevance: Useful as a semantic baseline, but not strong enough to deploy on its own.
models.benchmark_source: Recomputed from the Week 4 notebook design on the local dataset.

models.purpose: Evaluate a pre-trained transformer to see whether stronger language representations help on the business text template.
models.inputs: Text template only, tokenized for DistilBERT.
models.strengths: Strong modern NLP baseline with rich contextual text representations.
models.limitations: Text-only setup underuses the structured retail signals, and runtime is heavier than classical ML.
models.deployment_relevance: Useful for research comparison, but not the best fit for this label-driven retail dashboard.

deployment_choice.headline: Hybrid logistic regression was selected for deployment.
why_selected: It delivered the best Weighted F1 in the Week 4 comparison, which matters most for balanced Low / Medium / High decision quality.
It combines text context with numeric business signals, so it respects both semantic category information and operational reality.
Its coefficients, confusion matrix, and feature drivers are straightforward to explain in an academic demo.
It is stable to run inside FastAPI without GPU dependencies or heavier model-serving infrastructure.

selection_matrix.summary: Hybrid logistic regression achieved the strongest class-balanced benchmark score.
The custom deep learning model reached slightly higher raw accuracy, but not higher weighted F1.
Linear coefficients and simple preprocessing make the deployed model much easier to explain.
The deployed model fits a lightweight FastAPI workflow with minimal runtime dependencies.
Classical ML is easier to monitor, reload, and recover in a near-term demo environment.

deployment_choice.deployment_summary: The hybrid logistic regression model was deployed because it offers the best overall balance of business-aligned performance, interpretability, stability, and demo readiness.

compare_notes: The live comparison endpoint currently serves the three logistic-regression variants.
Custom deep learning and Hugging Face transformer results are shown from the saved Week 4 notebook benchmarks because no runtime artifact is currently attached.
For live comparison, any context fields not collected in the app form are filled from dataset medians or modes so the scenario remains stable and reproducible.
